mxCell.value: Child Device / Frontend
Cloud Run Backend Session Runtime
Gemini / ADK Bidi Runtime
Persistence, Assets, and Async Assembly
Optional Integrations / Legend
Read the core live loop left to right across the middle row. Lower rows show queued UI events, tool side effects, persistence, and movie assembly.

This page uses `RO-*` codes.
Other tabs: `Reconnect and Recovery (RC)` and `ADK Bidi Flow (BD)`.

RO-01 Browser storyteller client
StorytellerLive UI, scenes, assembly overlay, theater mode

RO-02 Browser audio engine
mic capture, PCM playback, page audio, SFX

RO-03 Browser websocket transport
WSS /ws/story + reconnect state machine

RO-04 UI rehydration
session_rehydrated, current image,
storybeat text, assembly status

RO-05 Browser-side Home Assistant apply
used for local/private lighting fallback

RO-06 FastAPI / ws_router
accept websocket, parse child events,
stream assistant output back

RO-07 Active websocket owner guard
connection_id, ghost close,
heartbeat watchdog

RO-08 ADK Bidi bridge
ResettableLiveRequestQueue + runner.run_live(...)
state mutation + reconnect restore

RO-09 Session event bus
buffered video_ready / lighting_command / theater_mode

RO-10 Storyteller tool suite
scene visuals, room lights, titles,
continuity, movie assembly

RO-11 Movie completion poll
wait for final MP4 and emit theater_mode
One current websocket owner per session_id prevents turn_complete, video_ready, and theater_mode from landing on different sockets.

RO-12 Storyteller ADK agent
shared prompt, turn policy,
tool choice, recovery prompts

RO-13 Gemini Live native-audio Bidi session
live audio in/out, streaming events,
function and tool calls

RO-14 Gemini helper models
image generation, continuity capture,
title generation, text repair
ADK Bidi is the live bridge: browser audio and control input go into a LiveRequestQueue, runner.run_live streams into Gemini Live, and streamed audio/tool events come back over the same live session.

RO-15 Tool outputs
story state, scene assets,
assembly request

RO-16 Firestore story/session mirror
resume state, story pages,
assembly status, release metadata

RO-17 GCS assets
scene stills, thumbnails,
page audio, final movies

RO-18 Cloud Run FFmpeg assembler job
reads assets and story pages,
writes MP4 and release payload
The final movie can appear after reconnect because storybook state is mirrored outside the live audio stream.

RO-19 Home Assistant lights
color, brightness, transition,
theater cues and wait-mode requests

RO-20 ElevenLabs
page read-aloud audio,
voice fallback, SFX

Legend
Blue edges: live browser/audio/control path
Green edges: backend orchestration + queued UI events
Gold edges: persistence and movie assembly
Gray dashed edges: optional integrations
Node lookup: see Voxitale_Runtime_Architecture_Dictionary.md
live WSS audio + control
HTTPS helpers /api/*
socket ownership + health
session state + live queue
invoke storyteller agent
ADK Bidi live stream
tool calls + tool responses
helper calls
publish UI events
flush queued events to current socket
theater_mode when movie is ready
story outputs
story state
scene assets
release metadata
read assets / write MP4
poll final movie
room lights
read-aloud / SFX
browser fallback
Browser / Frontend
Backend WebSocket Runtime
State / Event Queue
ADK / Gemini Live

This page uses `RC-*` codes.
See the other tabs for `RO-*` and `BD-*`.

RC-01 Transport/proxy closes
or page/tab resumes

RC-02 Frontend enters reconnecting
and keeps the current storybook UI visible

RC-03 Open a new websocket
with the same session_id

RC-04 Apply SESSION_REHYDRATED
restore image, storybeat text,
assembly status, scene history

RC-05 Resume mic only when safe;
otherwise remain in assembly wait mode
or passive story playback
RC-06 Backend accepts the new /ws/story connection

RC-07 Singleton guard swaps the active websocket owner
for this session_id

RC-08 Old ghost socket is closed
to prevent split delivery

RC-09 _prepare_clean_live_reconnect(...)
cleans up the prior live model state

RC-10 Restart _run_agent(...) and
_forward_session_events(...)

RC-11 Heartbeat ping + watchdog
close stale sockets if pong disappears

RC-12 replace_session_queue(session_id)
keeps buffered events alive across reconnect

RC-13 Cached storybook state + Firestore mirror
hold current scene and assembly progress

RC-14 Send SESSION_REHYDRATED
and restore_storybook_ui_after_reconnect(...)

RC-15 Drain queued video_ready, theater_mode,
lighting_command, and other custom events
to the current socket

RC-16 Late image completion or final movie readiness
can still arrive after reconnect

RC-17 Previous Gemini Live stream may clean-close
(for example 1000 cancelled)

RC-18 Fresh Gemini Live session starts
for the same conversation

RC-19 Pending child turn can be replayed
or recovered after render completion

RC-20 Conversation continues or assembly wait mode stays active
without losing the story state

Reconnect safety goals:
1. One active websocket owner
2. Preserve queued tool events
3. Rehydrate story + assembly UI
4. Restart live model cleanly
Ghost sockets are forcibly closed so image completion and turn completion do not land on different browser connections.
Buffered video_ready or theater_mode events can survive a fast reconnect because the queue is replaced, not discarded.
reconnect request
swap queue ownership
session_rehydrated + restored UI events
start fresh live stream
drain current queue
preserved events
video_ready / theater_mode / lighting_command
late completion still publishes
safe conversation resumes or assembly chatter continues
Browser / Child Device
Backend WebSocket Bridge
Google ADK Bidi Layer
Gemini Live + Tool Side Effects

This page uses `BD-*` codes.
See the other tabs for `RO-*` and `RC-*`.

BD-01 Child speaks into the browser
Mic frames captured as PCM

BD-02 Frontend sends audio and control JSON
over WSS /ws/story

BD-03 Frontend receives streamed assistant audio,
TURN_COMPLETE, video_ready, theater_mode

BD-04 UI plays Amelia's voice and updates
story image, captions, theater state

BD-05 ws_router receives websocket audio frames
and child JSON events

BD-06 _send_live_realtime(...)
wraps PCM as Blob and pushes realtime audio

BD-07 _send_live_content(...)
pushes text/control instructions when needed
(repairs, queued follow-up, recovery)

BD-08 _run_agent(...) multiplexes returned ADK events
into websocket audio + backend side effects
This backend is not just calling a model endpoint. It is acting as a live bidirectional bridge between the browser websocket and ADK's live request/event stream.

BD-09 ResettableLiveRequestQueue
real ADK LiveRequestQueue subtype
can discard stale frames on reset()

BD-10 runner.run_live(..., live_request_queue=live_queue)
opens the ADK live session

BD-11 ADK Bidi data plane
streaming requests in / events out

BD-12 Reset / recovery path
queue_reset, pending-turn replay,
clean reconnect after transport errors

BD-13 Gemini Live native-audio session
Bidi audio + text + tool calling

BD-14 Streaming output events
assistant audio chunks, transcripts,
tool/function calls, turn completion

BD-15 Tool side effects
scene generation, lighting, story assembly,
title repair, read-aloud

BD-16 Session event bus publishes
video_ready, lighting_command,
theater_mode back to the browser

Why ADK Bidi mattered here:
- true live audio conversation
- tool calls inside the same stream
- recoverable queue resets and reconnects
- browser stays synced with generated media
WSS audio + JSON
text/content lane
ADK Bidi session
streamed model events
events out
assistant PCM + JSON events
tool calls
publish UI side effects
video_ready / lighting_command / theater_mode
reset() after transport/model problems
clean reconnect / pending-turn replay
